discovery_instructions: Identify what the main item/entity is for this feature (e.g., employee record, document, invitation)
Look for list/table views - the rows represent the primary entity
Check page headers and breadcrumbs for entity naming
Note the singular and plural forms used in the UI

find_trigger: Look for buttons with: + Add, + Create, + New, plus icon
Check top-right corner of the page
Look in empty state messages for create prompts
create.validation: New item appears in list with unique identifier

find_trigger: Click on any row in a list/table
Look for 'View' or eye icon buttons
Check if rows are clickable (cursor changes)
read.validation: Detail panel, modal, or page opens showing full information

find_trigger: Look for Edit button, pencil icon
Check for inline editing (click on field to edit)
Look for status change dropdowns or buttons
Check action menus (three-dot menu) for edit option
update.validation: Changes persist after save, reflected in list view

find_trigger: Look for Delete button, trash icon
Check action menus (three-dot menu) for delete option
Look for bulk delete with checkboxes
Some features use 'Archive' or 'Cancel' instead of delete
delete.validation: Item removed from list or status changed to deleted/archived

instructions: Navigate to the feature's main section using the navigation_map
Take a full page snapshot to understand the layout
Find ALL clickable elements: buttons, links, tabs, dropdowns, icons
Map the navigation structure: tabs, sidebar items, breadcrumbs
Click each tab/sub-section and document what appears
Locate CRUD controls for the primary entity
Check for action menus (three-dot menus, right-click menus)
Document any modals, dialogs, slide-out panels
Identify form fields and input types when forms appear
Look for filters, search, sort, and export options
Check for bulk action controls (checkboxes, select all)
Note any settings or configuration options
discovery_targets: List/table of items with row actions

content_clusters.topic: Employee Leave Balance Visibility & Financial Impact
content_clusters.consolidation_notes: Three tickets addressing gaps in tracking leave balances across cycles, reporting on lapsed leave, and notifying users of expiration risks. Consolidated into single limitation around lack of persistent cross-cycle balance tracking and visibility.

Four tickets covering admin calendar UX, notification navigation, manager filtering, and bulk status updates. Consolidated into limitation around inefficient manager approval workflows and lack of granular filtering/bulk actions.
Single ticket on missing error messaging for leave requests spanning multiple cycles. Represents gap in user guidance for complex leave scenarios.

Three tickets addressing incomplete leave configuration process, missing documentation, and lack of mechanisms for handling complex policy scenarios (partial paid/unpaid, negative balance, policy updates). Consolidated into limitation around incomplete configuration framework.

Three tickets addressing lack of notifications when leave allowances change automatically per policy, missing reminders for salary configuration, and insufficient email communication on holiday updates. Consolidated into limitation around missing automated notifications for policy-driven changes.
Two tickets on tooltip persistence issue and missing attendance table refresh after leave request creation. Consolidated into limitation around UI rendering and data synchronization issues.

Four tickets addressing database constraints preventing leave cycle date updates, inability to change days in leave type edit dialog, missing backend support for days field updates, and lack of proration for one-time allowances. Consolidated into limitation around inflexible leave type and cycle configuration.
Single ticket addressing inability to reconcile work hours after half-day leave. Affects day-to-day attendance tracking and employee flexibility in managing partial leave scenarios.

detected_integrations.description: Leave requests trigger salary deductions and payroll calculations. System lacks mechanisms to notify HR when salary configuration is missing for unpaid leave employees, and employees cannot see deduction details from leave requests.
Leave management and attendance tracking are interdependent. Partial day leave scenarios require coordination between leave records and check-in/check-out functionality (AV-7407).

Leave management relies on notifications for approvals, balance expirations, and policy changes. Multiple gaps exist: missing expiration alerts, broken approval notification navigation, and absent allowance change notifications.
Leave management lacks integration controls with offboarding process. Offboarding employees can still request leave using negative balance, creating exit workflow risks.
Holiday updates affect existing leave requests. System lacks robust notification mechanism to communicate holiday changes and their impact to HR and managers.
Leave balance reporting depends on leave cycle data retrieval. Historical data constraints prevent accurate balance visibility in reports (AV-10078).

check_instructions: Navigate to Automations in the left sidebar
Click + Create workflow (or similar create button)
In trigger selection, search for this feature's name
Check if this feature appears as a trigger source
Document any trigger events found (e.g., 'item created', 'status changed')
Check Actions section for actions related to this feature
Look for 'Approval' or 'Pending Approval' status in the feature
Check if items have approval workflow indicators
Look for 'Submit for Approval' buttons or actions
Find approval settings in feature configuration
Document the approval chain (who approves what)
Test approval/rejection flow if test data available
Check for approval history or audit trail

what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The leave management system cannot persistently track leave allowances across multiple leave cycles, and lacks transparent reporting on leave balances that lapse due to carry-over policy constraints. Additionally, there is no automated notification mechanism to alert employees and HR about impending leave balance expirations, creating a risk of employees losing unused leave days without prior warning.

The leave management system lacks efficient manager approval workflows. The admin calendar design does not provide clear separation between viewing and action-taking functions, creating user confusion. Managers cannot filter leave requests by direct reports only, and there is no bulk status update capability—all changes must be made individually. Additionally, leave request approval push notifications do not navigate to the respective leave request screen, disrupting the approval workflow.

When employees or admins attempt to request leave that spans multiple leave cycles, the system fails to provide error messages or guidance on how to correctly submit the request. Users receive no feedback about the constraint or alternative approaches.

The leave management configuration process is incomplete and lacks comprehensive documentation. The system does not provide robust mechanisms for handling complex leave policy scenarios such as partial paid/unpaid leaves, negative balance carry-over, and flexible policy configuration updates. Configuration documentation is incomplete with evident gaps in standardized workflow documentation.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Automated Notifications for Leave Allowance Changes
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The platform lacks automated notification mechanisms for leave allowance modifications. HR managers are not notified when leave allowances change automatically according to defined policy conditions, requiring manual tracking and verification. Additionally, there are no reminders for HR managers to configure salary for employees who took unpaid leave, and email communication on public holiday updates affecting existing leave requests is insufficient.

The leave management system lacks flexible editing capabilities for leave requests, particularly during pending states. There are no clear mechanisms for managers to make corrections to leave request details or to change total days in leave requests for administrative corrections.

The leave management system lacks proper controls to prevent offboarding employees from requesting leave using negative balance. Employees in the offboarding process can still request and consume all remaining leave balance, potentially exploiting the system during their exit workflow.

The platform experiences UI rendering issues where tooltips in the leave balance card persist inappropriately during user interactions like scrolling or clicking. Additionally, the attendance module lacks automatic data refresh functionality after creating a leave request, requiring users to manually reload the page to see updated information.

The leave management system restricts users from working with historical dates and past leave cycles. Employees cannot submit retrospective leave requests, and the system cannot display leave balance information for periods before 2024 or for non-existent leave cycles. This prevents accurate historical reporting and blocks legitimate leave entries for past periods within active cycles.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Immutable Leave Type Configuration & Payroll Integration
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The leave management system does not allow modification of leave type characteristics (paid/unpaid status) after initial assignment, and lacks integration with payroll for automatic deduction calculations on unpaid leave. This creates a rigid configuration that cannot accommodate changing leave policies or complex payroll scenarios involving salary reductions.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Partial Day Leave & Work Hour Reconciliation
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The attendance system does not support flexible work hour reconciliation after partial day leave. Employees who take half-day leave cannot check in or record work for the remaining portion of the day, preventing dynamic time management and accurate attendance tracking for partial leave scenarios.

gaps_identified: Finance Manager has zero reported issues in this batch—no tickets directly address payroll calculation, settlement processing, or financial reporting gaps related to leave policies

execution_order: 2. EXPLORATION - Navigate to feature, discover all UI elements using exploration_phase instructions
3. IDENTIFY ENTITY - Determine the primary item/entity using primary_entity discovery
4. CRUD TEST - Test create, read, update, delete for the primary entity
5. WORKFLOW CHECK - Verify if feature has workflow triggers/actions
6. APPROVAL FLOW - Test approval process if present
7. WHAT_TO_DO - Execute each task from what_to_do array, validate expected outcomes
8. WHAT_TO_WATCH_OUT_FOR - Validate known limitations and edge cases
9. DOCUMENTATION - Capture screenshots of every significant state change
10. REPORT - Generate validation report with pass/fail for each task and issue
